did.physdesc: Correspondence, subject files, newspaper clippings, resumes, manuscripts, notes, reports, pamphlets, journals, books, ephemera, audiotapes, videotapes, photobooks.
did.abstract: The Brian R. McNaught collection documents McNaught's career as a sexuality activist, a church activist, writer, and sensitivity trainer. Topics include the controversy of gays in the Catholic Church, AIDS training and awareness, homophobia in the workplace, and gay rights. McNaught's works, books and videos include A Disturbed Peace, On Being Gay, Gay Issues in the Workplace, Growing Up Gay and Lesbian, Homophobia in the Workplace, and Now That I'm Out, What Do I Do?.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: Compiled by:
defitem.item: Brian R. McNaught, P. McCray, M.E. Warren, Melissa Kuypers, Brenda Marston

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Mireille Lee, May 2000 Sarah Keen, Ezra Corral, July 2008

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE ON BRIAN McNAUGHT
bioghist.p: Brian McNaught, one of seven children, was born to Waldo and Virginia Day McNaught on January 28, 1948, in Detroit, Michigan. He was educated in the Catholic schools, and at Marquette University, from which he graduated in 1970 with a degree in journalism.

McNaught's varied political and social activism includes his being a conscientious objector during the Vietnam War, protesting discrimination in the Catholic Church by going on a three week hunger strike, and serving as a spokesperson for gay and lesbian rights, especially as they pertain to the Catholic Church.

He has had careers as a newspaper columnist, writer, political appointee, gay activist, sexuality and AIDS educator, and corporate sensitivity trainer. McNaught has given numerous lectures and workshops at universities and corporations to help people gain a better understanding of gay and lesbian issues in the academic environment and the workplace.

McNaught served for two years, 1982-1984, as Boston Mayor Kevin White's liaison to the gay and lesbian community, the first full-time position of its kind. During this time, he worked with the mayor to write and implement the Boston Project, whose goal was to improve life for the city's gay and lesbian citizens. The project invited gay people to testify before city panels about how they were being treated. McNaught also established the first city government task force on AIDS in Boston in 1983.

As a newspaper columnist, McNaught wrote for several gay and Catholic publications, including the Michigan Catholic, the Blade, the Metro Gay Times, and Alive! magazine, as well as writing guest columns for several other publications. McNaught has also written books and produced videos. He has published On Being Gay, both as a book and video, and A Disturbed Peace, Gay Issues in the Workplace, Growing up Gay and Lesbian, Homophobia in the Workplace, and Now that I'm Out, What do I do? He also has seven educational DVDs on LGBTQ issues, three of which aired on TV stations across the country. His first video, A Conversation with Brian McNaught On Being Gay, was used in high school and college classrooms.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE ON RAY STRUBLE
bioghist.p: Ray Struble, born on June 18, 1951, in Wichita, KS, was one of seven boys to Art and Mary Gorman Struble. He initially pursued a religious path, studying to become a priest at Notre Dame University. After his novitiate year in Bennington, VT, he came out as gay, left the community, and became active in the early Masses celebrated by Fr. Paul Shanley for the gay community in Boston, joining Dignity/Boston and serving as Vice-President.

Professionally, Struble worked in various roles, starting as a food and beverage manager and bank teller before moving into the financial sector. He made significant strides in the industry, eventually becoming the Managing Director of Global Equity Sales at Lehman Brothers before retiring at 45. He was one of the first openly gay people on Wall Street. Struble earned his B.A. from Boston College by attending night school.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Publications removed from collection 7590 and cataloged as individual books and serials in the Human Sexuality Collection are listed at the end of this guide under Separated Materials.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Boxes 1 (folders 13-29), 2, 19, 23 Series III. Speaking Engagements and Workshops
Boxes 3-5 (folders 1-36), 23 Series IV. Catholic and Unitarian Universalist
Boxes 17-20, 23 Series IX. Visual Materials and Ephemera
Boxes 21-22, 23 Series X. Audio and Video Materials

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Brian R. McNaught papers, #7590. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

acqinfo.head: PROVENANCE
acqinfo.p: Brian McNaught gave the first materials in this collection to the Cornell University Library in January 1995. Photographic albums delivered by Jean McGlynn, a friend of the couple, who lives in Skaneateles, NY, in August 2020.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, subject files, newspaper clippings, resumes, manuscripts and notes, audiocassettes and videocassettes, photographic albums and other material documenting McNaught's career as a sexuality activist, a church activist, writer, and sensitivity trainer, dating from 1974 to 2011. Topics include the controversy of gays in the Catholic Church, AIDS training and awareness, homophobia in the workplace, and gay rights. Includes numerous letters from appreciators and detractors of McNaught's work, newspaper articles about McNaught, and copies of his books and video programs. Also includes a daily diary kept during a hunger strike and civil rights battle with the Catholic Church in Detroit, 1974. In addition to the Catholic Church, organizations figuring prominently in the collection include The National Council of Churches, the United States Catholic Conference, the Call to Action, Catholics for Gay Rights, Dignity, the American Association of Sex Educators, Counselors, and Therapists, the Workshop on Sexuality at Cazenovia, and the Boston Mayor's Committee on AIDS. Publications in which McNaught appears include The Michigan Catholic (in which his column "Write On" appeared), and Esplanade. McNaught's works, books and videos, include A DISTURBED PEACE, ARE YOU GUYS BROTHERS?, ON BEING GAY, GAY ISSUES IN THE WORKPLACE, GROWING UP GAY AND LESBIAN, HOMOPHOBIA IN THE WORKPLACE, and SEX CAMP. Correspondents include Malcolm Boyd, Barbara Gittings, Elaine Noble, David McWhirter, and Tim McFeeley.

Also, reports, pamphlets, journals, books, and ephemera on social and political aspects of sexuality, homosexuality, and AIDS. Materials and final report of The Boston Project, "a year long study by the City of Boston, in conjunction with the Gay and Lesbian Community, of how we might better meet the needs of this often neglected constituency," for which McNaught served as Liaison to the gay and lesbian community; resources McNaught used for developing speeches and presentations; CDC's "Business Responds to AIDS: Manager's Kit"; cassette labeled "Thornfield 1989 Brian"; two videocassettes (one of McNaught, the other a gay cruise promotional video), and three envelopes of letters from relatives and friends.

Included 3 t-shirts from campaign of openly gay congressman Barney Frank; an early gay liberation play on the state motto, "Gays Make it in Massachusetts"; and "Make Love, Not Breeder Babies," which was created for his video "On Being Gay". More videos and audiocassettes of McNaught speeches and television appearances, and other gay rights, gay Catholic, and gay workplace issues, including Gov. Dukakis signing the Massachusetts Gay Rights Bill on Nov. 15, 1989,and Catholic Bishop Thomas Gumbleton's speech about his gay brother, "My Brother Dan." Also, more personal and business correspondence, including with the Human Right Campaign, Paul Shanley, the Salvation Army, the Syracuse University professor in clinical-psychology, and sex educator Sol Gordon (1923-2008).

In Sub-series IX.B. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photographic Albums, 41 annotated photobooks, taken and arranged chronologically by Ray Struble, document the lives of Brian McNaught and Ray Struble from the beginning of their relationship in Brookline, MA, in May 1976 to 2004. The annotations are in the hand of Brian McNaught (enclosed in each album), providing the context of each album. An 11-page document (Box 27, Folder 1, or in digital format), written by the donor, offers detailed descriptions and comments on each one of those albums, including the people who appeared in the photographs and biographical notes for both McNaught and Struble. Researchers could request this document's digital or analog version for detailed descriptions. In the existing inventory, briefly highlighted information for each album is provided. For McNaught, photo albums ended when iPhones and iCloud replaced cameras and leather-bound albums. Researchers could explore gay life through the lens of the couple, including home life, family and friend gatherings, all the Marches on Washington, the Quilt, Civil Unions, same-sex marriage, Provincetown life, the first-ever gay cruise to Greece and Turkey, and down the Yangtze, professional and personal trips, and parties. The albums include pictures of Brian at "Sex Camp," the Workshop in Sexuality at Thornfield, an Episcopal retreat facility on Lake Cazenovia in Cazenovia, NY (albums 4,8,14-15, 17,22). Brian was a speaker and staff member there for 30 years. Among his primary teachers was Dr. Bill Stayton, a noted sexologist and theologian in the American Baptist Church (albums 17 and 22). Albums 37-41 contain photographs of the McNaught and Struble families, photographs taken by Brian's parents and assembled into an album as a college graduating gift, 6 x 9 and 8 x 10 photographs that do not fit the formats of the chronological pictures (albums 1-36), and press photographs kept by Brian McNaught from his work as layout editor of Metro Gay News in Detroit. Notable people in these photographs include Elaine Noble (album 4-5), the openly gay woman elected to the Massachusetts House of Representatives in 1974; Gerry Studds (albums 5,7,18), the first openly gay man to be elected to the U.S. House of Representatives; Fr. Paul Shanley (album 1,2,5,8,9,32), Boston street priest assigned to work with gay people, and later imprisoned in the Catholic Church sex scandal; Sr. Jeannine Gramick (albums 5, 13, 28, 32, 33), co-founder of New Ways Ministry; Drs. David McWhirter and Drew Mattison (albums 7, 13, 20), authors of the groundbreaking study and book, The Male Couple; Tomie de Paola (albums 5,8,9), beloved children's book author and illustrator, Dr. Sol Gordon (albums 4,8,9,17,37), Syracuse University professor of human sexuality and founder of the Annual Workshop on Sexuality at Thornfield ("Sex Camp"), Ann Maguire (album 23, 25, 31, 32), early, out lesbian in Boston, and manager of the bar, Somewhere. Ann followed Brian as the Mayor's Liaison to the LGBTQ Community.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Boxes 14-16 and 24 restricted until 2049.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to two folders of letters in Box 24 restricted until 2033.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the personal records of Brian McNaught from 1965 - 1998. Included in the series are diaries kept while on a hunger strike in 1974 in protest of being fired from a Catholic publication for writing about being gay; documents from his filing as a conscientious objector during the Vietnam war; address books; materials from his high school and college, and photographs.

did.unittitle: Daily diary kept during Michigan Catholic Ordeal
File pertaining to McNaught's marriage in Canada to his partner, Ray Struble.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos of Brian, Ray, homes, Thornfield "sex camp"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Naples Daily News request for an article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Honoring Brian with Legacy Award 2019

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains articles and manuscripts written by Brian McNaught between the years 1969 - 1993. Included are drafts of his books On Being Gay, and A Disturbed Peace. Also included are his weekly articles "A Disturbed Peace," and "Write On!," originally written for The Michigan Catholic; and other articles written by McNaught for various publications. Other writings include an unpublished children's story Grogg the Frog; a monograph manuscript, When Irish Eyes are Gay, and a published letter to Anita Bryant: "Late Night Thoughts of a Catholic Homosexual."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contracts with St. Martin's Press for "On Being Gay," "Gay Issues in the Workplace," and "Now That I'm Out, What Do I Do?" and assorted contracts

did.unittitle: Series III. Speaking Engagements and Workshops

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials from workshops and lectures given by McNaught between the years 1979-2003. The bulk of the lectures were at various colleges on the subject of being gay and Catholic, and the workshops primarily deal with homophobia in the workplace. Of note are the papers from homophobia workshops at the ATT/League, a workshop series for employees of ATT designed to raise awareness about gay and lesbian issues and make ATT a more comfortable environment for gays and lesbians.

did.unittitle: American Association for Affirmative Action (AAAA) - Boston
American Association of Sex Educators, Counselors and Therapists (AASECT) conference
Subseries III a. ATT/League homophobia workshops (co-directed by McNaught and Mary Lee Tatum)
Right Wing Family Research Council Response to ATT, LEAGUE, promoting homosexuality, etc.
Independent School Health Association (ISHA) - Loomis-Chaffee School
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force - Conference
New England School of Alcohol Studies
William Patterson College of New Jersey
Society for the Scientific Study of Sex (SSSS)- Regional Boston
Promotion on Workshop and Homophobia on Campus
Various "Gay Issues in the Workplace" Workshop Evaluations
Articles and Newsletters concerning Gay/ Catholic issues
GLBT Employee Business Resource Groups of NCR Materials (League @ NCR)
WILL Global Professional Development Conference, Boston, MA
City College of San Francisco: Spring Professional Development
WILL (Women In Leadership at Lucent Technologies) Conference
Notes on Out and Equal Conference presentation with Deb Dagit (Merck) and Kathy Marvell (Chubb) on "Sexual Health in Corporations"
"Understanding and Managing Gay and Transgender Issues at Chubb"
Flyer, "Manager's guide for gay and transgender workplace issues"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unique web resource produced by Diversity Guides that corporations would license for their internal intranet. Customers incluced Microsoft, Chubb, Merck, SC Johnson, and others.

did.unittitle: "Understanding and Managing Gay and Transgender Issues in the Workplace", A Facilitator's guide
Series IV. Catholic and Unitarian Universalist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Brian McNaught's files that deal directly with Catholic issues and the Catholic Church. Of note are the papers and newsletters about Dignity, a national organization for gay Catholics. There are also files about the Michigan Catholic, the newspaper from which he was fired in 1974. This series also includes articles pertaining to gay Catholics and newsletters from various Catholic organizations and churches, both local and national. Also included is a file on McNaught's involvement with the Unitarian Universalist Church in Naples, FL.

did.unittitle: Articles and Newsletters concerning Gay/Catholic issues (includes some Dignity)
Dignity - an organization for gay Catholics

did.abstract: Folders 44-54 labeled "Dignity" include various newsletters, correspondences and other information concerning Dignity. May contain other gay/Catholic material as well.

did.unittitle: Gay/ Catholic issues - includes Salvatorian Gay Ministry
Michigan Catholic: Legal Documents on Case
did.physloc: Oversize folder located along the side of Box 5
Oversize folder located along the side of Box 6

did.unittitle: National Task Force of Gay People in the Church
Welcoming Congregation- Unitarian Universalist Church, Naples, FL
"Baptism and the Experience of 'Coming Out'." 6th National Symposium on Catholocism and Homosexuality [contains two audio CDs]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Brian McNaught's files from his time as the Liaison to the mayor for the gay and lesbian community, 1982-1984. Included in this series are testimony and materials from the "Boston Project," started by mayor Kevin H. White and designed to improve life in Boston for the gay and lesbian community. Also included are various files about Boston, such as police files, local events, and a file on the Beantown softball league.

did.unittitle: The Boston Project - "toward an agenda for gay and lesbian citizens"- transcripts
Boston Project - Letter to Gay and Lesbian Community from Brian McNaught
Subseries V.a. Files from when Brian McNaught served as the Liaison to the Gay and Lesbian Community in Boston for Mayor Kevin H. White, 1982-1984
Boston Intercollegiate Gay and Lesbian Alliance (BIGLA)
Boston Lesbian and Gay Political Alliance (BLGPA)
GLAD - Gay and Lesbian Advocates and Defenders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Brian McNaught's files relevant to his work. Included in this series are his files on AIDS information, files on specific people and gay and/or Catholic organizations such as the National Gay Task Force, the Thornfield workshop, and two Marches on Washington. There is information on a variety of gay and lesbian issues in the form of reports and articles.

did.unittitle: AIDS - Manager's Kit - Responding to AIDS: Workplace Policy, Employee Education, Resources
AIDS Patients - Adam and Phil (and more)
American Society for Sex Educators, Counselors and Therapists (AASECT)
Eastern College Students' Reaction Papers to a McNaught Speech
Exodus from the Closet by Patrick Scanlon (manuscript)
Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation (GLAAD)

Sad Dilemma of the Gay Catholic

Sanger Award - "for distinguished service in the field of Responsible Sex"

Communication from LOGO on gay programming
Gay-related newsletters and workplace guides from Stonewall in London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of personal letters and other correspondence received by Brian McNaught from 1965-1998. There are letters from bishops and priests, heads of Dignity chapters, and personal letters from friends, as well as business correspondence. Of note are statements of support from numerous people during his hunger strike, including the letter ending his 1974 fast. Also included in this series is fan mail about his various books as well as letters from friends and family. Some letters have been RESTRICTED UNTIL 2049 or 2033 by the donor.

did.unittitle: Subseries VII.a. General Correspondence and Letters
Michigan Catholic Hunger Strike - Statements of Support
On Being Gay - Letters about book
Letter from Ray to Lehman Boss inviting Brian to speak

Fan Mail/General and Personal Correspondence [ RESTRICTED]

Struble, Ray - cards and letters to and from
"Names You Might Know" (includes Bishop John Sprong, Carson Kressley, Gregory Maguire, Sol Gordon, Jeannine Gramick, Joan Garry, Kevin Jennings, and others)
Proposed book on Manager's Guide to Gay and Transgender Issues
Gay workplace mail [relating to work in corporations on gay and transgender issues]
Rejection letters on Are You Guys Brothers?
College student responses to book Sex Camp. [required reading for 500 students at the University of Maine]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Support Documents include articles about McNaught and reviews of his writing; also, magazines, brochures, and pamphlets McNaught saved that are relevant to his work. Some publications were separated to be cataloged as part of the Human Sexuality Collection print resources. See: Separated Materials.

did.unittitle: Christopher Street, Sep. 1979; CCPA News and Views (Newsletter of Catholics for Christian Political Action), Nov. 1978; Probe, Mar. 1978, vol. "Coming Out- A Gay Guide to Notre Dame and South Bend," Fall 1977; "How can You Tell if You're Really in Love?" 1980; "A Proposal for the Prevention and Correction of Delinquency Among Youth," 1965; "The Myths of Homosexuality," Nov. 1973; "The Truth Seeker," July/Aug. 1989; Newsweek, June 6, 1977; The Witness, Feb. 1976; Oct. 1977; Oct. 1978; June 1979; Oct. 1979
The Advocate, issue 559, Sept. 11, 1990; Training and Development, Jan. 1992; "Liberty and Justice for All: A Discussion Guide" Dec. 1974

Ladies Home Journal, Dec. 1980; "Planned Parenthood Resource Review," vol. 5, no. 1, 1986-87; Psychology Today, Jun. 1986; Ms., Dec. 1979; "Behind Closet Doors: The Door Looks at Homosexuality," The Wittenburg Door, no. 39 Oct/Nov. 1977

"Analysis of the 96th Congress," The Gay Rights National Lobby, 1980; Spirituality Today, vol. 30, no. 2, June 1978; "Celebration '90 - Gay Games III and Cultural Festival" Official Program, Vancouver, Aug. 4-11, 1990; "Maklary's Digest," no. 2, Dec. 16, 1981 "Standing Commission on Human Affairs and Health," 1980

"How Can You Tell if you're Really in Love?" 1980; American Educator, Fall 1978; "One in 10: A profile of Alaska's Lesbian and Gay Community," 1986; press release re: Massachusetts Attorney General James M. Shannon's 1987 Executive Order prohibiting discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation, AIDS/HIV status (also related articles); Free Inquiry, vol. 1, no. 1 Winter 1980/81; "The AIDS Action Committee of Massachusetts: the Next Decade," 1992; "Ten Heavy Facts about Sex," 1971; "Help Yourself: a self-help handbook by and for the students of the University of Maryland," 1979; "Sex in a Plain Brown Wrapper: By the students, for the students," Syracuse University, 1976

"The Consequences of Being Gay: A Report on the Quality of Life for Lesbian, Gay and Bisexual Students at the University of Massachusetts at Amherst," June 1985l "Homosexuality: (Part I) What Science Understands, (Part II) Before Counseling Gays and Lesbians," 1987; "The Gay and Lesbian Speakers Bureau Manual," Jan. 1984

"How to be a Trainer: A Self-Instructional Manual for Training in Sexual and Reproductive Health Care," Planned Parenthood of Maryland, 1980; "Speaking for Ourselves: Training for the Gay and Lesbian Speakers Bureau of Boston," n.d.

Tommy's Connection, vol. 1, no. 18 n.d.; The Watchtower, Aug. 15, 1974; Marquette Tribune, April 25, 1975, April 30, 1975, May 5, 1975, Nov. 28- Dec. 1, 1989; Lifestyle Boston, Nov. 27, 1979; High Gear, Nov. 6, 1981; Gay News, Sept. 4-17, 1981; Gay Liberator, April- May 1975; Gay Liberator, Feb.- March 1975; Entertainment West, n.d.; Civil Liberties, Nov. 1977; Commonweal, May 24, 1974; Bondings, Fall 1985 and Sept. 29, 1978, Sept. 30, 1978, Oct. 17, 1978, June 12, 1979, Oct. 1, 1979, Oct. 2, 1979; The Michigan Catholic, vol. 108, no. 1 Jan. 4, 1980; A.D. (After Detroit) 1977; On the Edge: A Catholic Worker Newspaper, vol 3, no. 1, Jan. 20, 1978; Commitment, Jan. 1979; The National Catholic Reporter, March 8, 1974 (vol. 10, no. 19), Nov. 14, 1975 (vol. 12, no. 4), June 18, 1976, Nov. 5, 1976 (vol. 13, no. 3), Oct. 21, 1977 (vol. 13, no. 46), Nov. 11, 1977 (vol. 14, no. 4), Nov. 18, 1977 (vol. 14, no. 5), Jan. 27, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 14), Feb. 10, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 16), March 3, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 19), March 10, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 20), May 19, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 30), June 2, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 32), June 16, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 33), Aug. 18, 1978, Oct. 13, 1978 (vol. 14, no. 42), Nov. 21, 1980 (vol. 17, no. 5); Southern Cross, May 29, 1975
Series IX. Visual Materials and Ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are various posters, and some advertisements for McNaught lectures. Also old calendars, buttons, two Boston Gay Pride hats; slides and photographs of McNaught from the 1970s. Included in this box is a film strip/tape presentation entitled "The Hidden Minority: Homosexuality in Our Society."

did.unittitle: Subseries IX a. Photographs and Ephemera
Slides and Photos for video "On Being Gay"

Posters advertising McNaught Lectures; Calendars from 1979-1980; Records, Filmstrips and Tapes from "The Hidden Minority: Homosexuality in our Society" n.d.; Lesbian and Gay Pride Day - Boston hats 1983; Miscellaneous Buttons and Pins; Metropolitan Detroit Science Fair ribbon 1962; Cassette tape and Lyrics from Lynn Lavner n.d.; 15 boxes of slides of McNaught; Record cover "Jungle Drums - Wild Fantasy"; Miscellaneous Posters and Drawings; Book covers for On Being Gay
Nametag for XVII World Congress of Sexology
Introductory Training on Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender (LGBT) Inclusion in USAID's Workplace and Programming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To be broadcast February 17, 2011

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 41 photographic albums, taken and arranged by Ray Struble chronologically. Albums 37-41 contain photographs too large (8x10) to fit in chronological format. The scope and content notes for each album in this inventory provide highlighted information. For detailed descriptions and comments on the albums by Brian McNaught, see Box 27, Folder 11, or request a digital version of this document.

did.unittitle: Albums' description by Brian McNaught and Ray Struble
1. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brookline apartment (1983); McNaught's home in Birmingham, MI (1977); Dignity/Boston party; Fr. Paul Shanley; Reverent Nancy Wilson at Denver Metropolitan Community Churches (MCC) Conference; Family gatherings. Boston Marathon (1979);

did.unittitle: 2. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Struble's birthday; friends and family gatherings on several occasions; Deerfield Fair; cutting Christmas tree at Trappist monastery. Fr. Paul Shanley and Fr. Matthew Flynn.

did.unittitle: 3. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McNaught family gatherings on several occasions (Christmas, birthdays, wedding anniversary); Deerfield Fair; New gay friends in Puerto Rico; Walden Pond.

did.unittitle: 4. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grandmother McNaught's cottage torched; Christmas tree cutting and party; a trip to California; Charlene Gaynor's visit; "Sex Camp," with Dr. Sol Gordon, and Mary Lee Tatum; Brookline apartment visit from Elaine Noble, Lisa Myers; Deerfield Fair; Walden Pond; visit Trappists; Gerry Studds.

did.unittitle: 5. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McNaught grandmother's Christmas, visit to Tomie de Paola in Wilmot Flat, New Hampshire; New Year's with Gerry Studds; Donors cabin on Franklin Pierce Lake in Hillsborough, NH; Elaine Noble at Boston Gay Pride; With friends at cabin on Pierce Lake; 1983 Boston Gay Pride, AIDS Action Committee; Struble's birthday at the lake; Alison Deming.

did.unittitle: 6. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary Lee Tatum; McNaught and Struble's house in Gloucester, before and after renovations, "Bachelors' Hall"; McNaught cousin reunion; and family.

did.unittitle: 7. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gloucester, visitors to Bachelors Hall – Reverent Bill Johnson (first openly gay person in the US to be ordained by a mainline Protestant denomination, United Church of Christ) and Sandy Brawders; David McWhirter and Drew Mattison; Christmas party in Bachelors Hall – among others joined Richard Burns (NY Gay Community Center, GCC), Tim McFeeley (Human Right Campaign, HRC), Stephen McCauley (author – Object of My Affection). McNaught family Christmas gathering, trip to St. Barths.

did.unittitle: 8. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: St. Barth's; 10th Anniversary celebration at Bachelors Hall; Tomie de Paola; Tim McFeeley; McNaught as Grand Marshall of Detroit Gay Pride; "Sex Camp" ; Fr. Paul Shanley and the staff of his church visit Gloucester; March on Washington; Quilt; McNaught's award from HRC in Boston; The Human Rights Campain Fund event (1988).

did.unittitle: 9. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McNaught in Hawaii at a conference organized by Gloria Blum and Sol Gordon, includes Betty Dodson (the G-spot), Toby Simon (Brown University), Fr. Paul Shanley; Bob Struble family visits Gloucester, Tomie de Paola, NH auction, Walker Hancock (sculptor) visits Bachelors Hall. Christmas party at Bachelors Hall; Drew Mattison in California home; the Rev. Bill Johnson; Dr. Eli Coleman; Gay Games in Vancouver; Family.

did.unittitle: 10. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pool party in Gloucester – among others, Larry Kessler (gay Catholic activist who started the AIDS Action Committee in MA), Drs. Doug Hughes and Terry Keane; Thanksgiving: going away party for transfer to Atlanta; David Mills (openly gay judge in Danvers, MA); McNaught family visit to Gloucester

did.unittitle: 11. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Move to Atlanta, renovate house, friends.

did.unittitle: 12. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stone Mountain; pool party; historic RSVP cruise to Greece and Turkey, Toga Night party on board with friends; Struble's parents visit Atlanta; AIDS Quilt in Washington, D.C.

did.unittitle: 13. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: LGBTQ March and Quilt; Drew Mattison and Dave McWhirter, Larry Kessler, Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, Tim McFeeley; visit with Sr. Jeannine Gramick; a trip to Italy with gay brothers.

did.unittitle: 14. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Atlanta home; Gay Pride in Atlanta, pool party afterwards; "Sex Camp"; P-town; Atlanta pool party; trip to Ireland; with friends to Costa Rica.

did.unittitle: 15. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trips to Costa Rica, San Francisco, river rafting in Georgia, "Sex Camp," Colorado with Struble family and cruise to Alaska.

did.unittitle: 16. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alaska trip; Planned Parenthood Sexuality educators visit house in Atlanta; Christmas in New York; trip to France with gay brothers.

did.unittitle: 17. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trip to France with gay brothers (continued); "Sex Camp"; Brian and Ray's 25th Anniversary, Central Park; trip to Costa Rica.

did.unittitle: 18. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provincetown; Gerry Studds and Dean Hara; visit to Bob Struble and sons in Kansas; AIDS Quilt in Washington, D.C.; move to San Francisco.

did.unittitle: 19. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Renovated house on Baker Street, in San Francisco. Monty Collins interior design and project manager.

did.unittitle: 20. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: San Francisco – David McWhirter and Drew Mattison; Rick Flinn and Dr. Jim Braude; Donald Adamian; Family visiting for Easter; Brian's birthday whale watching.

did.unittitle: 21. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provincetown; Family and friends. Whale watching; P-town Drag Brunch; San Francisco Christmas; Steven Tierney; Cross-country travel; Fourth of July in P-town; Mick Ellis; Eric Marcus and Barney Karpfinger.

did.unittitle: 22. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sex Camp" staff retreat in P-town. Dr. Bill Stayton, Alison Deming, Carol Dopp, Pam Wilson, Susan Vasbinder, Linda Roessler; Carnival; Drag Bingo; Ginger Vidas; touring cross country back to San Francisco; Ahwahnee Hotel; 1998 move to Naples (FL).

did.unittitle: 23. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Millennium March in DC; Naples (FL) friends; Provincetown – David Mitchell as Scarbie, Brian as judge at Carnival, playing Mexican Train.

did.unittitle: 24. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provincetown; Bob Struble and sons; Christmas in Naples (FL); Jerry Chasen; Pam Wilson and Richard Simms; Jack and Jean McGlynn; Naples house.

did.unittitle: 25. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Naples friends; Tom McNaught and husband (to be) Matthew Gilbert (Boston Globe TV reviewer) visit; Vermont Civil Union on Brian and Ray's May 4th 25th Anniversary, Jack Coleman presiding (former head of Philadelphia Federal Reserve and President of Haverford College), Ann Maguire and Harriet host special dinner for Brian and Ray, their brothers and friends; Struble nephews.

did.unittitle: 26. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jean McGlynn's birthday; Friends; trip to Ghana and South Africa with Naples Art group.

did.unittitle: 27. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Safari in South Africa.

did.unittitle: 28. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Naples friends; Wichita, KS, Mary Struble's 90th birthday party; Provincetown Fourth of July lane party hosted by Bob McCamant and Jack Croucher; Sr. Jeannine Gramick.

did.unittitle: 29. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Naples Halloween; Thanksgiving; renovation and move to Provincetown quarters house; Mary Struble's funeral, family gathers; marriage in Ottawa; Christina Bresani, Mike Struble, join Bob and boys for Fourth of July in Provincetown; Talk on LGBTQ issues in the workplace in London by Wall Street firms.

did.unittitle: 30. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Touring Wales and Scotland; Home of the Ladies of Llangollen; Thanksgiving in New York; Tom Roberts' parents join for Christmas in Naples; Lord of the Rings birthday part; Oscar party.

did.unittitle: 31. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oscar's party continued: Gatherings with friends and family on several occasions (weddings and birthday parties).

did.unittitle: 32. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McNaught cousins visit Provincetown; Carnival with David Mitchell as Scarbie; Fr. Paul Shanley and Sr. Jeannine Gramick; Historic gay male couples cruise in Yangtze River, sponsored by American Express.

did.unittitle: 33. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Move from Naples to Fort Lauderdale (FL); Sr. Jeannine Gramick; Larry Wald and Scott Belding; Bob Struble and sons visit Fort Lauderdale home for Easter; Trip to Amsterdam.

did.unittitle: 34. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Visit Tom Roberts' birthplace in Canfield, OH, and David McChesney's birthplace in Pittsburgh; game night in P-town home; trip to Portugal for Christmas, after London presentation; South Haven at Christmas; Kayak the Galapagos in Ecuador, hike the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu in Peru.

did.unittitle: 35. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Machu Picchu; Venice after London presentation; Hong Kong for presentation, then Vietnam and Cambodia.

did.unittitle: 36. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preparation for Carnival parade in P-town. End of the chronological story-telling.

did.unittitle: 37. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs too large (8x10) to fit in a chronological format; McNaught family photographs and cards; Childhood; keynoting at 1981 American Association of Sexuality Educators, Counselors and Therapists (AASECT) Convention in San Francisco; Grandma McNaught's cottage on Pleasant Lake; Naples house; Mary Lee Tatum, Alaska trip; March on Washington with gay brothers.

did.unittitle: 38. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs too large (8x10) to fit in a chronological format; Brian's headshots and casual; At the City Hall plaza, photograph taken for book Positive Gay Images; press photographs of Gregory Baum (pro-gay Catholic theologian); Craig Russell (impersonator of Judy Garland, film Outrageous); Jack Baker and Mike McConnell (featured in Look magazine dedicated to "The American Family"); Jimmy Carter White House historic meeting of LGBTQ leaders with Midge Costanza; Boston Gay Pride; Bachelors Hall in Gloucester.

did.unittitle: 39. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs 8x10 – professional photographs in Bachelors Hall for book; membership photograph for the Unitarian congregation in Naples; Ray's First Communion class; novitiate class; Art and Mary Struble wedding.

did.unittitle: 40. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs 8x10 – McNaught family photographs from significant occasions (parents; wedding, father's graduation, Brian's grandmother with her Brannigan siblings, and more), childhood, and other siblings.

did.unittitle: 41. Brian McNaught and Ray Struble Photo Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album created by Brian's parents with Brian's childhood, graduation from Marquette University, student report cards, correspondence, a summer in Frances, dressed for Model UN delegate from the UAR, Field Day at Brother Rice, news clippings of Brian receiving Leadership Award, of the Junior Prom, of a college dorm, Charlene Gaynor, frat house, yearbook co-editor announcement.

did.unittitle: Series X. Audio and Video Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains videos and tapes made by McNaught to be used primarily as educational tools, as well as to help people struggling with their own sexual identity.

did.unittitle: New Ways Ministry: Growing Up Gay and Catholic - McNaught
Human Sexuality - Brian McNaught, Nov. 5 1980 - Part I
Human Sexuality - Brian McNaught, Nov. 5 1980 - Part II
Brian McNaught Radio Interview, KNUS-Denver, Apr. 20 1982
Briggs, Philip and Sidney Brown, Herb Stone
"Defamation," narrated by Harvey Fierstein for the Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation
McNeill Lecture, Nov. 20, and Questions and Answers
Congressman Gerry E. Studds, University of Illinois, Urbana-Champagne, May 4 1986
Ron Wesner and Dick York interview for WITNESS, Sep. 21
Artists, Gay and Lesbian - Introductions - Tape 1: Overview
Artists, Gay and Lesbian - Tape 2: Morning Session
Artists, Gay and Lesbian - Tape 3: Providers
Boston Project I and II, Police Lt. McNells - Debriefing, Discussion
Boston Project III, Steven Tierney/IV, Joseph D'Onofrio
Gay/Lesbian Disabled Citizens and City Services I and II
Gay/Lesbian Disabled Citizens and City Services III
Gay/Lesbian Elderly and City Services I and II
Gay/Lesbian Elderly and City Services III
Education and Information about Lesbians Roundtable - the B[?] Project
Education and Information about Lesbians Roundtable - the B[?] Project (cont.)
Women's Roundtable/guests Brian McNaught, Fr. Kenneth Baker, Sister Jeannina Gramick
Lou Gordon program: "Are Homosexuals Going to Hell?"
Health and Hospitals Roundtable - Tape I
Health and Hospitals Roundtable - Tape II
Health and Hospitals Roundtable - Tape III
Health and Hospitals Roundtable - Tape IV
Health and Hospitals Roundtable - Tape V
Lesbian Health Issue Roundtable Panel - Tape 1
Lesbian Health Issue Roundtable Panel - Tape 2
Stupek Show Aug. 15 1974 I and II
Stupek Show and McNaught WDET Aug. 1 1974
Stupek Show Aug. 15 1974 IV and V
Gay and Lesbian Minorities Roundtable I - Introduction: Overview/discussion/homophobia/racism
Gay and Lesbian Minorities Roundtable II - Relational legitimacy/immigration
Gay and Lesbian Minorities Roundtable III - Discussion/recommendations
Gay and Lesbian Minorities Roundtable IV - Wrap-up
Neighborhood Hearings Area I Mon. nite July 25
Neighborhood Hearings Area I Mon. nite July 25
Police Panel: Goldsmith, Gottschalk and Barry I: G/L as Victims of Violence
Police Panel: Victims of Violence - J.B.
Police Panel III Tape One - Chuck Wexler/Charles Maplethorpe
Police/Community Relations II - Barry, Johnston, Vaiciulus
Police Safety/Legal Panels G/L Roundtable - Tape 4
Prison - After-Introductions - Specific issue brainstorming - I
I Youth Hearings: Gen. Dev. Overview: Ron Wozniak, SJ/SR Barbara [?]/Alan Tweedy - Adoption/Ed Roche
II Youth panel May 24, Human Services 4. Group Proc and Project Aware
II Youth Panel Human Services "Spec. Threats" - 1. George Smith May 24 1983, GAA/Ken Smith/G/L Hotline 5. May 24
II Youth Panel Human Services 2. Paul Robinson - BCH/Kevin Cranston 5-24 6.
II Youth Panel Human Services 3. Ellen Haper-M.L. of Comm. Health Cmrs.
Gay Issues in the Workplace - McNaught
"Gay and Lesbian People in the Workplace: What are the Issues?" by Brian McNaught
Signing of the Massachusetts Gay Rights Bill by Gov. Dukakis
"Being Gay at Work" by Brian McNaught
LEAGUE Conference: Pat Russo and Brian McNaught
Gay and Straight: Our Common Ground. Presentation to Goldman Sachs
Presentation to New Ways Ministry Conference
"Brian McNaught, 2004" DVD of Brian's youth prepared for his 60th birthday by his brother Tom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brian's TV debate with Anita Bryant Ministries

did.unittitle: "A conversation with Brian McNaught on being gay"
"Understanding and Managing Gay and Transgender Issues in the Workplace"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video preview
